revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Aotsuka Kofun]][[ja:青塚古墳 (犬山市)]][[simple:Aotsuka Kofun]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Inuyama, Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Aotsuka Kofun | native_name =青塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Aotsuka Kofun (Aichi).JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_size = | alt = | caption = Aotsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Aotsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.326397|N|136.928942|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Inuyama|qid=Q503411|1=Inuyama, Aichi}} | region = {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|qid=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes, on site museum | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|19|34|N|136|55|45|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}}
The {{nihongo|'''Aotsuka Kofun'''|青塚古墳 |||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Aotsuka neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|qid=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi|1=Inuyama, Aichi}} in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|qid=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was Historic Site1 a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201886|title=青塚古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=}}</ref> It is the second largest ''kofun'' found in Aichi Prefecture after the [[Danpusan Kofun]] in Nagoya. ==Overview== The Aotsuka Kofun is located in the southern part of Inuyama, in western Aichi Prefecture. It is one of tumuli which once typeed a ''kofun'' cluster in the surrounding area (most of which have now been destroyed). [[Archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted in 1979 and from 1995 to 1998.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 123 meters and is orientated to face southwest. It consists of a three-tiered posterior circular portion with a diameter of 78 meters and height of 12 meters, and a two-tier trapezoidal anterior portion, with a width of 62 meters, length of 45 meters and height of seven meters. A surviving portion of a moat is on the west side of the tumulus, and on the east side a raised embankment for crossing the now vanished portion of the eastern moat has also survived. An unusual feature of this ''kofun'' is the presence of a rectangular mound on the far end of its anterior portion, which may have been a ceremonial plattype. The tumulus was typeerly covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' across its entire surface, and had rows of cylindrical and drum-shaped ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''. It is believed to have been built in the middleof the 4th century in the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The ''kofun'' is associated with the ''Ni-no-miya'' of {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|qid=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}}, {{ill|Ōagata Shrine|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōagata Shrine|qq=}}, which is located 3.5 kilometers to the east. The shrine claims that the ''kofun'' is the grave of its ''kami'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命|||qq=}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} during the 1584 {{ill|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|en|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|qid=Q1353633|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|1=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The site is now open to the public with the exterior of the tumulus restored to its appearance at the time of construction, with a row of replica pot-shaped ''haniwa'' as part of the Aozuka Kofun Historical Park , which also has an on-site museum. It is located about a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Gakuden Station (Aichi)|en|Gakuden Station (Aichi)|qid=Q5517700|lt=Gakuden Station|1=Gakuden Station (Aichi)}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|en|Meitetsu Komaki Line|qid=Q6788786|lt=Meitetsu Komaki Line|1=Meitetsu Komaki Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==Oagata Shrine== {{Main|Ōagata Shrine|qq=}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Oagata Shrine | religious_affiliation = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Oagata Shrine Hime no Miya Plum Orchard - 2018.jpg | caption = Hime no Miya and Umezono | deity = {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命|||qq=}}<ref name="Shiseki" /> }} [[File:Inuyama Oagata Plum Blossoms Over Shrine Rooftops.jpg|thumb|A plum orchard made on a slope.]] [[File:Ōagata-jinja_Himeishi.jpg|thumb|293x293px| Himeishi]] '''Ōagata Shrine''' (大縣神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|qid=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}.<ref name="Shiseki" /><ref name=":0" /> It is the [[:en:Ninomiya (shrine)|Ninomiya]], or a second Ranked shrine in {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|qid=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}},<ref name="Shiseki" /> Ranked below [[Masumida Shrine]].<ref>{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher=Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese|qq=}}</ref> It is located close to [[Tagata Shrine]] (田縣神社) the shrine famous for hosting the "penis festival" [[Hōnensai]]. Oagata shrine has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB|qq=}}</ref> Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of the shrine, 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].<ref name="Shiseki" /> The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命|||qq=}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} during the 1584 {{ill|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|en|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|qid=Q1353633|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|1=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}}.<ref name="Shiseki" /> The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|qid=Q9652090|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== {{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
[[file:Aodzuka Kofun air.jpg|thumb|right|Aerial view of Aotsuka Kofun]]
